feature.summary: Settings > Providers import/export with secret-free default export, optional high-friction secret export, side-effect-free import preview, explicit conflict handling, and no active/default provider transfer.

known_risks: Raw-vs-masked provider API key behavior must be resolved during implementation design; export redaction must be server-owned.
planning_gate.project_cognition_status: blocked: project-cognition.db missing; live repository evidence used

must_preserve.obligation: Provider configuration sharing remains the goal.
Import/export covers saved provider records only.
Users may choose whether to export secrets.
Secret export is separate, dangerous, second-confirmed, not remembered, and clearly labeled.
Conflict default is add/rename; overwrite is explicit.
Active/default provider selection is not exported or imported.
Import preview with diagnostics occurs before commit.
Workflow import/export is process precedent only.
Server-owned redaction resolves raw-vs-masked key evidence conflict for export safety.

consequence_obligations.claim: Default export must not include credentials; optional secret export is explicit only.
consequence_obligations.validation: Server tests assert secret-free bundle lacks raw keys/tokens.

consequence_obligations.claim: Import preview must be side-effect free.
consequence_obligations.validation: Server tests assert provider index and settings unchanged after preview.

consequence_obligations.claim: Import/export must not carry active/default provider selection or mutate active runtime env.
consequence_obligations.validation: Server tests assert activeId and managed env unchanged.

consequence_obligations.claim: Conflict handling defaults to add/rename; overwrite is explicit.
consequence_obligations.validation: Server and desktop tests cover default resolution and explicit overwrite.

consequence_obligations.claim: New imports use generated IDs by default.
consequence_obligations.validation: Server tests assert generated local IDs differ from source IDs.

consequence_obligations.claim: Resolve raw-vs-masked API key behavior by making export redaction server-owned.
consequence_obligations.validation: Tests prove export redaction independent of provider list response shape.

consequence_obligations.claim: Secret export uses high-friction protection and credential-bearing artifact labeling.
consequence_obligations.validation: Server and desktop tests cover explicit confirmation and labeling.

server: src/server/types/provider.ts or colocated provider import/export schema module

forbidden_drift: Do not implement raw Settings JSON as the main provider sharing path.
Do not export/import activeId or default provider selection.
Do not make secret export a remembered checkbox.
Do not rely on frontend-only masking for export security.

dispatch.reason: Native subagent spawning is visible but not authorized by the current tool contract without an explicit user subagent request; artifact-only planning proceeded leader-inline.
